key_highlights: Global bond selloff deepens with yields surging, as markets price in rate hikes rather than cuts, driven by inflation fears and new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's hawkish stance.
Stocks post worst day since March, with tech and chipmakers hit hard by rising yields; Dow retakes 50,000 but volatility remains high.
Cerebras Systems IPO soars 68-90% in year's biggest IPO, signaling strong AI demand, while Applied Materials raises outlook on semiconductor equipment growth.
Berkshire Hathaway sells $8 billion of Chevron shares and amasses $2.6 billion stake in Delta Airlines, signaling sector rotation.
Mortgage rates surge toward 8%, adding pressure on housing and consumer spending, while business prices spike ('Tabasco hot') fueling inflation concerns.
LIRR workers strike for first time since 1994, disrupting commuter rail, while an electrical fire adds to service suspensions.
Trump-Xi summit continues with no progress on Iran, contributing to global sell-off; rare Ebola strain in Congo adds geopolitical risk.
AI data centers employ very few people, raising questions about job creation, while Ford's AI-driven rally abates after 15-month high.

sectors.trend: Global bond selloff deepens with yields surging; 10-year Treasury yield tops 4.5%, mortgage rates near 8%. Markets price in rate hikes, not cuts, as new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh takes over. SocGen says yields becoming 'unhinged' amid inflation fears.
sectors.implications: Higher borrowing costs across the board, pressuring equities, housing, and corporate debt. Risk of further selloff if inflation data remains hot. Defensive positioning in short-duration bonds and TIPS warranted.

sectors.trend: Stocks post worst day since March; Dow retakes 50,000 but volatility spikes. Tech and chipmakers lead declines as rising yields drive selloff. Emerging markets also hit hard, worst week since March on inflation fears.
sectors.implications: Risk-off sentiment dominates; defensive sectors (consumer staples, healthcare) may outperform. Value stocks with strong cash flows could be resilient. Monitor for further downside if bond yields continue to rise.

sectors.trend: AI trade roars back with Cerebras IPO soaring 68-90%, Applied Materials raises outlook on semiconductor equipment demand. However, chipmakers dumped as rising yields drive selloff. Nvidia extends winning streak on China news, but AI data centers employ very few people.
sectors.implications: AI remains a key growth driver but valuations are stretched. Rising yields could pressure high-growth names. Focus on companies with strong fundamentals and real revenue (e.g., Applied Materials, Nvidia). Cerebras IPO success signals investor appetite for AI hardware.

sectors.trend: Oil prices rise, spooking bond investors and adding to inflation fears. Berkshire sells $8 billion of Chevron shares, while hedge funds boost bullish copper wagers to five-month high. Alcoa invests $65M in low-carbon aluminum. YPF seeks $25 billion oil project incentive.
sectors.implications: Energy sector benefits from higher oil prices but faces regulatory and transition risks. Copper demand from electrification and AI data centers supports bullish outlook. Berkshire's Chevron sale suggests profit-taking; consider diversified commodity exposure.

sectors.trend: LIRR workers strike for first time since 1994, disrupting commuter rail; electrical fire adds to service suspensions. Fuel costs driving demand to buses. Berkshire amasses $2.6 billion stake in Delta Airlines, signaling airline recovery. Ford falls after AI-driven rally abates.
sectors.implications: Transportation disruptions could impact regional economies. Airlines may benefit from travel demand but face fuel cost headwinds. Berkshire's Delta stake is a bullish signal for the sector. Ford's AI pivot may be overhyped; monitor fundamentals.

sectors.trend: Mortgage rates surge toward 8%, pressuring housing affordability. Empire State Realty Trust announces dividend, but Zillow CEO prepares for next real estate revolution. Global bond selloff adds to headwinds.
sectors.implications: Housing market faces significant headwinds from high rates; homebuilder stocks and REITs may underperform. However, demographic demand and limited supply could provide a floor. Monitor mortgage applications and home sales data.

sectors.trend: Pepsi and Colgate-Palmolive highlighted as best stocks to buy in falling markets. Barclays raises price targets on Performance Food Group and Primo Brands. Monster Beverage also gets price target raise from Morgan Stanley.
sectors.implications: Defensive sectors are favored in risk-off environment. Consumer staples with pricing power and stable demand offer safe havens. Food and beverage companies may benefit from inflation hedging. Consider adding to positions.

sectors.trend: MP Materials highlighted as best American mining stock; Hecla Mining and Pan American Silver get buy ratings. US needs another decade to fix rare earth crisis. Copper bullish bets at five-month high.
sectors.implications: Critical minerals and rare earths are strategic priorities; companies like MP Materials could benefit from policy support. Silver and gold miners may hedge against inflation and currency debasement. Copper demand from electrification and AI is a long-term tailwind.

sectors.trend: Eton Pharmaceuticals Q1 earnings call; Lucid Diagnostics, Fennec Pharmaceuticals, Alpha Cognition report. Rare Ebola strain with no approved vaccine kills dozens in Congo, raising biosecurity concerns.
sectors.implications: Healthcare sector offers defensive characteristics. Biotech companies with novel therapies could see volatility. Ebola outbreak may boost vaccine developers but also poses geopolitical risk. Monitor for government contracts.

sectors.trend: Forbright files for IPO pitching middle market and digital banking. ADX welcomes Morgan Stanley as first international remote trading member. Dollar notches best week since early March on Fed hike view.
sectors.implications: Rising yields benefit banks' net interest margins but could increase credit risk. Digital banking IPOs may attract interest. Strong dollar pressures emerging markets but supports US financials. Monitor loan growth and credit quality.

key_themes: Global bond selloff and rising yields dominate markets, with inflation fears and hawkish Fed driving rate hike expectations.
AI and semiconductor demand remain strong, but valuations are vulnerable to rising rates; Cerebras IPO highlights investor enthusiasm.
Geopolitical risks: Trump-Xi summit with no Iran progress, rare Ebola outbreak in Congo, and LIRR strike add uncertainty.
Sector rotation from growth to value and defensive plays; Berkshire's moves signal caution and opportunity in airlines.
Housing market under pressure from surging mortgage rates; consumer spending may slow as business prices spike.
Commodity supercycle themes: copper, rare earths, and energy remain in focus amid supply constraints and electrification demand.
Corporate earnings: mixed results with some beats (Applied Materials, SSR Mining) and misses (Xanadu, Ford).
AI data center job creation questioned; Ford's AI pivot seen as overhyped.
Subscription fatigue and consumer spending shifts noted; coffee company employs homeless as social impact trend.
SpaceX IPO filing expected soon, adding to IPO market momentum.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios; favor floating-rate notes and TIPS. In equities, rotate to value and defensive sectors with strong cash flows. Consider hedging with put options on growth ETFs.

Increase exposure to commodities (gold, copper, energy) and inflation-protected securities. Favor companies with pricing power (consumer staples, healthcare). Reduce exposure to long-duration bonds and high-growth tech.

Diversify geographically; increase exposure to US and developed markets. Monitor oil prices and safe-haven assets (gold, USD). Avoid direct exposure to conflict zones. Consider biotech for Ebola vaccine plays.
Avoid direct exposure to affected regional economies. Consider alternative transport stocks (buses, airlines). Monitor for broader labor unrest.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Housing Market Slowdown / Mortgage Rate Surge
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to homebuilders and REITs. Favor rental housing and property management companies. Monitor mortgage REITs for dividend cuts.
Take profits on high-flying AI stocks with no earnings. Focus on companies with real revenue and margins (Applied Materials, Nvidia). Avoid speculative IPOs until after lockup expiration.
Diversify across sectors; favor companies with strong balance sheets and consistent dividend growth. Monitor earnings calls for forward guidance. Use stop-losses on volatile names.
Hedge foreign currency exposure in international portfolios. Favor US-based companies with domestic revenue. Emerging market debt and equities may underperform.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to defensive consumer staples (Pepsi, Colgate-Palmolive, Monster Beverage) for downside protection.
opportunities.rationale: These stocks are highlighted as best in falling markets by Wall Street analysts, with pricing power and stable demand. Barclays and Morgan Stanley raised price targets, indicating confidence.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy semiconductor equipment stocks (Applied Materials) on pullback for AI and chip demand growth.
opportunities.rationale: Applied Materials raised outlook, and Cerebras IPO success underscores AI hardware demand. Rising yields may cause short-term volatility, but long-term fundamentals are strong.

opportunities.recommendation: Add to copper and rare earth miners (MP Materials, Freeport-McMoRan) for electrification and AI data center demand.
opportunities.rationale: Hedge funds boost bullish copper bets; US needs to fix rare earth crisis. MP Materials is highlighted as best American mining stock. Alcoa's investment in low-carbon aluminum supports the theme.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider buying Delta Airlines on Berkshire's $2.6 billion stake as a recovery play.
opportunities.rationale: Berkshire's large stake signals confidence in airline recovery. Fuel costs are a headwind, but travel demand remains strong. Berkshire's track record suggests value.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in gold and silver miners (Pan American Silver, Hecla Mining) as inflation hedges.
opportunities.rationale: TD Securities upgraded Pan American Silver; H.C. Wainwright maintains buy on Hecla. Rising yields and inflation fears support precious metals. Dollar strength may cap gains, but long-term outlook positive.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to long-duration bonds and high-growth tech; increase cash and short-duration TIPS.
defensive_moves.rationale: Bond yields are 'unhinged' and likely to rise further with hawkish Fed. Tech stocks are vulnerable to higher discount rates. Cash provides optionality to buy dips.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge equity portfolios with put options on SPY or QQQ to protect against further selloff.
defensive_moves.rationale: Stocks posted worst day since March, and further downside is possible if yields continue to rise. Options provide asymmetric protection.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to homebuilders and mortgage REITs; favor rental housing and property managers.
defensive_moves.rationale: Mortgage rates surging toward 8% will crush housing affordability and demand. Rental demand may increase as homeownership becomes less accessible.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid speculative AI IPOs until after lockup expiration; focus on established players with earnings.
defensive_moves.rationale: Cerebras IPO soared but may face profit-taking. AI data centers employ few people, questioning job creation narrative. Ford's AI rally abated, showing hype can fade.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase exposure to US dollar and reduce emerging market exposure.
defensive_moves.rationale: Dollar notches best week since early March on Fed hike view. Emerging markets had worst week since March on inflation fears. Strong dollar will pressure EM assets.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish with high volatility. Bond yields likely to continue rising as markets price in rate hikes under new Fed Chair Warsh. Equities face headwinds from higher discount rates and inflation fears. Defensive sectors and commodities may outperform. Geopolitical risks (Trump-Xi, Iran, Congo) add uncertainty. Key level to watch: 10-year yield above 4.5% could trigger further equity selloff. Mortgage rates near 8% will slow housing. Cerebras IPO success may be a peak for AI hype.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously optimistic. AI and semiconductor demand remain structural growth drivers, but valuations need to adjust to higher rates. Commodities (copper, rare earths, energy) benefit from electrification and supply constraints. Defensive consumer staples and healthcare provide stability. Housing may bottom if rates stabilize. Berkshire's moves (selling Chevron, buying Delta) suggest sector rotation. Inflation may moderate if oil prices ease, but sticky services inflation remains a risk. Fed policy will be key.
key_catalysts: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first policy speech and FOMC meeting minutes.
Trump-Xi summit outcomes, especially on trade and Iran.
May CPI and PCE inflation data releases.
Cerebras and other AI IPOs' aftermarket performance.
Berkshire Hathaway's 13F filing for full portfolio details.
SpaceX IPO filing and potential valuation.
Ebola outbreak containment and vaccine development.
LIRR strike resolution and impact on regional economy.
Oil price trajectory amid geopolitical tensions.
Housing market data (existing home sales, mortgage applications).
watch_list: Bitcoin and crypto as risk sentiment indicator
Consumer confidence and retail sales data
